# Introduction

Chronic diseases remain the foremost contributors to mortality and morbidity in the United States and account for over $90\%$ of the nation's annual healthcare costs \cite{CDC_Chronic_Disease}. Effective management of chronic conditions has been demonstrated to mitigate healthcare costs \cite{meyer2008chronic} and is intrinsically linked to one's ability to access the healthcare system and its services \cite{nguyen2011peer}. Despite advancements in healthcare delivery, there remains existing healthcare access disparities which are exacerbated by growing demand during crises \cite{lopez2020parks, boz2024investigating}. Pharmacies serve as an extension of the healthcare system in the US and are regarded as important amenities in a neighborhood, serving as access hubs for food, counseling, and other ancillary services that may fall outside the purview of traditional physician-provided care \cite{DiasFernandes2022, black2010neighborhoods, neckerman2009disparities}. Consequently, public health researchers may be interested in measuring a neighborhood's access to pharmacies as it relates to epidemics, policy, or health conditions that rely on one's ability to interact with pharmacies.

## Background

The conceptual framework for healthcare access was extensively delineated by Penchansky and Thomas in 1981, who identified multiple dimensions of access: accessibility, availability, affordability, acceptability, and accommodation \cite{Penchansky1981}. Accessibility pertains to the physical proximity of services, availability refers to the volume and capacity of services, affordability encompasses the economic barriers related to health insurance and income, acceptability involves the sociocultural compatibility between providers and patients, and accommodation addresses the appropriateness and responsiveness of services to patient needs.

Methods used to measure pharmacy access vary. A scoping review by Fernandes et al. revealed a predominance of proximity-based approaches in measuring pharmacy accessibility \cite{DiasFernandes2022}. An example of how proximity is measured would be the distance or travel time from the center of a neighborhood to the nearest pharmacy \cite{DiasFernandes2022}. These measures are typically calculated using straight-line distance, the Haversine formula, or, more recently, through a road network facilitated by Geographic Information Systems (GIS) tools like ArcGIS. In addition to proximity, density measures constitute the second most common method for assessing pharmacy access \cite{DiasFernandes2022}. Density is generally operationalized as the ratio of available pharmacies to the population within a defined areal unit. Additionally, there is a body of work that has utilized ideas from the food desert literature to define pharmacy access in terms of a pharmacy desert. A pharmacy desert is a neighborhood without a pharmacy within 1 mile from its center or within half a mile with low access to vehicles and low income \cite{Pednekar2016, Pednekar2018, Wisseh2021, Qato2014}.

Limitations in how proximity methods are operationalized, are based on the assumption that individuals utilize the nearest pharmacy but that does not account for competition based on varying population sizes (demand) \cite{Ikram2019}. Additionally, the basic density methods mentioned before are not able to capture the behavior of individuals residing at neighborhood boundaries who may utilize pharmacies outside their immediate locales. Catchment based methods like the 2SFCA method emerged as a popular method when measuring access to other health care aspects but is underutilized when measuring pharmacy access \cite{DiasFernandes2022}. Moreover, there is no consensus on how to measure pharmacy access among investigators in terms of what thresholds, distance, or travel times to use, and therefore conclusions about pharmacy access remain variable. This is as a result of low data availability, familiarity with GIS tools, and ultimately understanding how individuals actually travel to pharmacies, which may vary by region. In light of this, Ikram et al. proposed a catchment size that serves as a baseline of expected pharmacy proximity within a region. That is, the travel time that 96% of the population is to their nearest pharmacy. This proposed method is used in this study in New York City along with a weighted linear combination of walking and driving.

## Objective

This study aims to address the following gaps in the existing literature: (i) First, the conventional approach of using the nearest pharmacy from the neighborhood centroid as a proxy for individual travel behavior lacks empirical validation, particularly in selecting appropriate catchment sizes for the implementation of the 2SFCA method. Additionally, the 2SFCA method has yet to be explored using various modes of transportation to compare pharmacy access within NYC.

Describe and characterize census tract level pharmacy density by using two stage floating catchment area method (2SFCA) that combines information on proximity to pharmacies as well as population that access those pharmacies with respect to user defined catchments around the centroid.

-   AIM 1a: Determine the best catchment size to use in the 2SFCA method for census tracts in NYC;
-   AIM 1b: Propose a linear weighted combination procedure for pharmacy density that accounts for multiple modes of transportation.

# Methods

## Data Sources and Study Variables

In this retrospective cross-sectional study, all population centered census tracts (CTs) in New York City (NYC) will be included based on the 2020 Decennial Census \cite{census2020centers} outline with a population of over $150$, and we will exclude those labeled as “parks” or “cemeteries.” Population data for individuals aged $16$ years or older at the CT level, categorized by primary mode of transportation—driving, public transportation, or walking to work—will be sourced from the American Community Survey (ACS)\cite{socialexplorer2020} as $5$-year estimates. Additionally, the 2020 Social Vulnerability Index (SVI) will be obtained from the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC)\cite{atsdrsvi}, that utilized $5$-year estimates from the ACS to create a composite score derived from sixteen social determinants of health. These variables are organized into four overarching categories: socioeconomic status, household characteristics, racial and ethnic minority status, and housing and transportation \cite{hassett2024scoping}. Each census tract, across the US, receives a rank as $1$ if in the top $10\%$ ($90$'th percentile or higher) among the 16 variables. Then that same process is repeated among the four categories after summing the ranks from the individual variables to produce an overall vulnerability score \cite{atsdrsvi2020}.

### ACS variables

Data variables are located using [socialexplorer.com](https://www.socialexplorer.com/data/ACS2020_5yr/metadata/?ds=SE&var=A09005_006). Using this website I am able to identify the name of variables and the codes they are indexed as in order to retrieve them when using the census api.

```{r}
#| eval: true
census_key <- "da78adf0d44806a4957cf7805559f170ba2c69e7"

acs_vars <- c(
  
  total_trans = "B08301_001",
  car_truck_van = "B08301_002",
  walked = "B08301_019"
  

  
  
)


acs_check1 <- str_detect(acs_vars, '_0[0-9][0-9]$')
if (any(acs_check1==FALSE)) {
    cat(acs_vars[which(acs_var_check1==FALSE)], '\n')
    stop('PLEASE REVIEW ACS VARIABLE IDS or add underscore before last 3 numbers')
}
# PULL ACS DATA
############################################
acs_raw <- tidycensus::get_acs(
    survey='acs5',
    variables=acs_vars,
    geography='tract',
    state=c('NY'),
    year=2020, #changed from 2019 to 2020 because new development may have include more island residence.
    key=Sys.getenv('census_key'),
) %>%
    rename_all(str_to_lower) %>%
 # select(-moe) %>%
  group_by(geoid, name) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    values_from = c("estimate","moe"),
    names_from = "variable"
  ) %>%
  filter(substr(geoid,1,5) %in% c(36005,36047,36061,36081,36085,
                                  
                                  36059, #"Nassau County"NY
           36119 #"Westchester County",NY
                                  ))

acs_raw %>% 
  ggplot()+
  geom_histogram(aes(x = estimate_car_truck_van/estimate_total_trans, fill = "Proportion Driving"), alpha = 0.3)+
  geom_histogram(aes(x = estimate_walked/estimate_total_trans, fill = "Proportion Walking"), alpha = 0.3)+
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2", name = "Mode of transportation")+
  labs(x = "Proportion", y = "Count")


```

Pharmacy location data within NYC will be utilized from the NYU Langone Epic database, originally compiled for a broader cohort study \cite{adhikari2023cohort}. Pharmacy addresses were geocoded using Geocodio \cite{geocodio} in the R statistical software \cite{R2024} to derive longitude and latitude coordinates corresponding to each address. Latitude and longitude coordinate points of pharmacies and population-weighted centers of census tracts (centroids)\cite{census2020centers} will be used to measure proximity, i.e., distance or travel time, between locations through a road network. Measures of proximity are obtained using an open-source routing machine (OSRM)\cite{osrmr, osrm} that provides precise routing information based on OpenStreetMap\cite{openstreetmap}. Pharmacy availability will be measured as the number of pharmacies in a census tract (CT).

## Derived Variables

In the absence of actual individual travel behavior to pharmacy, arbitrary catchment sizes are chosen as five, ten, and fifteen minutes of driving and walking time. Additionally, a travel time representing the time it takes $96\%$ of the population to reach the nearest pharmacy will serve as our primary catchment size, as defined by Ikram et al. \cite{Ikram2019}.

Similarly, distances in miles are chosen as half a mile, one mile, and the distance that $96\%$ of the population is from the nearest pharmacy. This will result in twelve catchments and corresponding pharmacy density distributions in total.

### Shapefile

The difficulty here is that shore files exist for limited regions. This makes it difficult to visualize since the polygons include water areas. In this process I was able to include the polygons that are clipped to shore for New York City but State level census tracts will include water areas. The census tracts out side of NYC are only included so that we can consider their values when neccessary to account for border effects.

```{r Shapefile}
#| eval: true

shore_shp <- sf::st_read("./data/nyct2020_23d/nyct2020.shp") %>%  
                                    mutate(
# Creating county codes                                    
                                           county = case_when(BoroCode == 2 ~ 36005,
                                                              BoroCode == 3 ~ 36047,
                                                              BoroCode == 1 ~ 36061,
                                                              BoroCode == 4 ~ 36081,
                                                              BoroCode == 5 ~ 36085),
# Creating geoid                                 
                                           GEOID = str_c(county,CT2020)) %>% 
                                  mutate(GEOID = as.numeric(GEOID)) %>% 
# Defining projection options 
                                  #  sf::st_transform(., "+proj=longlat +datum=WGS84") %>%  
                                    sf::st_transform( 2263)%>% 
  mutate(
# determining the center of polygons
    lat = sf::st_coordinates(sf::st_centroid(.))[,2],
    lng = sf::st_coordinates(sf::st_centroid(.))[,1]
  )


invisible(ct_ny = tigris::tracts(state = "NY",year = 2020))

pop_cen_nyc = readr::read_delim("./data/censtract_weighted_pop_2020.txt") %>% 
  janitor::clean_names() %>% 
  
# cleaning longitudinal variable
  separate(longitude, into = c("longitude","x"), sep = "\\\\$") %>% 
  select(-x) %>% 
  
# creating geo_id
  mutate(geoid = paste0(strokec2_statefp,countyfp,tractce),
         longitude = as.numeric(paste0("-",sub("^-0", "", longitude)))) %>% 
  select(strokec2_statefp,geoid,latitude,longitude,population) %>% 
  mutate(county = substr(geoid,1,5)) %>% 
  # filters to NYC counties and counties that are connected by land
  filter(county %in% c(36005,
                 36047,
                 36061,
                 36081,
                 36085
         ))


pop_centroids = readr::read_delim("./data/censtract_weighted_pop_2020.txt") %>% 
  janitor::clean_names() %>% 
  
# cleaning longitudinal variable
  separate(longitude, into = c("longitude","x"), sep = "\\\\$") %>% 
  select(-x) %>% 
  
# creating geo_id
  mutate(geoid = paste0(strokec2_statefp,countyfp,tractce),
         longitude = as.numeric(paste0("-",sub("^-0", "", longitude)))) %>% 
  select(strokec2_statefp,geoid,latitude,longitude,population) %>% 
  mutate(county = substr(geoid,1,5)) %>% 
  # filters to NYC counties and counties that are connected by land
  filter(county %in% c(36005,
                 36047,
                 36061,
                 36081,
                 36085,
                 36059, #"Nassau County"NY
           36119 #"Westchester County",NY)
         ),
         population >=150) %>% 
  mutate(tot_pop = sum(population))

map_shp = rbind(
  shore_shp %>% 
    filter(!grepl("cemetery",NTAName, ignore.case = T)) %>% 
    select(GEOID,geometry) %>% 
    mutate(county = substr(GEOID,1,5)) %>% 
    st_transform(crs = 2263),
  ct_ny %>% 
    select(GEOID, geometry) %>% 
     mutate(county = substr(GEOID,1,5)) %>% 
  # filters to NYC counties and counties that are connected by land
  filter(county %in% c(
                 36059, #"Nassau County"NY
           36119 #"Westchester County",NY)
         )) %>% 
    st_transform(crs = 2263)) 

map_shp %>% # checking if neighboring county ct's are included
  plot()

```

```{r Centroids}

#| eval: true
pop_cen_nyc = readr::read_delim("./data/censtract_weighted_pop_2020.txt") %>% 
  janitor::clean_names() %>% 
  
# cleaning longitudinal variable
  separate(longitude, into = c("longitude","x"), sep = "\\\\$") %>% 
  select(-x) %>% 
  
# creating geo_id
  mutate(geoid = paste0(strokec2_statefp,countyfp,tractce),
         longitude = as.numeric(paste0("-",sub("^-0", "", longitude)))) %>% 
  select(strokec2_statefp,geoid,latitude,longitude,population) %>% 
  mutate(county = substr(geoid,1,5)) %>% 
  # filters to NYC counties and counties that are connected by land
  filter(county %in% c(36005,
                 36047,
                 36061,
                 36081,
                 36085
         ))



 #  ggplot()+
 #  
 # # geom_sf(data = map_shp, aes(fill = 10000/population ), col = "gray90")+
 #    geom_sf(data =pharm_sf, col = "red", alpha = 0.3 )+
 #  coord_sf(xlim = c(-74.3,-73.6), ylim = c(40.5,40.9))+
 #  scale_fill_viridis_b()
  
map_only = 
  tigris::geo_join(
  map_shp,
  pop_centroids, # used to include population weighted centroids
  by_sp = "GEOID",
  by_df = "geoid",
  how = "inner") %>% 
  filter(county.x %in% c(36005,
                 36047,
                 36061,
                 36081,
                 36085,
                  36059, #"Nassau County"NY
           36119 #"Westchester County",NY)
           )
           )

map_only %>% plot()

map_labels <- 
            paste0(
                "Tract: ",
                map_only$GEOID, "<br/>",
                "SES: ",
                map_only$population
                
                ) %>%
            lapply(htmltools::HTML)

map_pop_pal <- leaflet::colorQuantile(
                     domain = map_only$population, 
                               n= 5,
                               palette = "Reds", 
                               alpha = T,
                               na.color = "#808080")


map_only %>% 
  sf::st_transform(crs = 4263) %>% 
leaflet() %>% 
  addProviderTiles(providers$CartoDB.Positron) %>% 
  addPolygons(
    fillColor = ~map_pop_pal(population),
    fillOpacity = 0.7,
    color = "gray",  # Border color
    weight = 0.5,    # Border weight
    opacity = 1,
    highlightOptions = highlightOptions(
      weight = 2,
      color = "blue",
      fillOpacity = 0.9,
      bringToFront = TRUE
    ),
    label = map_labels,
    labelOptions = labelOptions(
      style = list("font-weight" = "bold", "color" = "black"),
      textsize = "12px",
      direction = "auto"
    )
  ) %>% 
  addLegend(
    pal = map_pop_pal,
    values = ~population,
    title = "Median Value Owner Un-imputed",
    opacity = 1,
    position = "topright"
  )


  ggplot()+
  geom_sf(data = map_only )+
  geom_sf(data = pharm_with_tract, size = 0.01, color = "green", alpha = 0.5)+
    theme_void()
```

### Data Join

```{r}
#| eval: true
# joins pharmacies and populations by geoid

pharm_with_tract <- st_join(pharm_sf, ct_ny[, "GEOID"] %>% sf::st_transform(sf::st_crs(pharm_sf)))

data1 = left_join(pop_centroids,
          pharm_with_tract %>% sf::st_drop_geometry() %>% 
            rename("geoid"= "GEOID"), 
          by = "geoid") %>% 
  # indicating a census tract that has a pharmacy or not
  mutate(ind = if_else(is.na(longitude) | is.na(latitude), 0,1),
         tot_pharm = sum(ind)) %>%# filter(ind == 1) %>% 
  #creating pharmacy count and density
   select(-c(name, longitude, latitude)) %>% # removing pharmacy specific data to avoid duplicate values
  group_by(geoid) %>% 
  mutate(
         pharm_count = sum(ind),
         pharm_den = pharm_count/population*10000) %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  unique() %>% 
  mutate(
         E = (tot_pharm/tot_pop*population),
         SIR = pharm_count/E ) %>% 
  left_join(acs_raw,
            by = "geoid") 

data1 %>% 
  ggplot()+
  geom_histogram(aes(x = SIR), fill= "orange", alpha = 0.3)+
  geom_histogram(aes(x = pharm_count), fill = "blue", alpha = 0.3)+
  geom_histogram(aes(x = pharm_den), fill = "green", alpha = 0.3)+
  labs(x = "pharmacy measures")


data_map = tigris::geo_join(
  map_only,
  data1,
  by_sp = "GEOID",
  by_df = "geoid"
)

  
adjacency_list = get_adj(map = data_map,
                           style = "queen",
                           order = 1,
                           dist_threshold = NULL)

n_copy = 100
moran_ps <- numeric(n_copy)
sapply(1:n_copy, function(i){
  
  res_null = try(inla(
      pharm_count ~ 1,
      family            = "poisson",
      data              = data_map %>% sf::st_drop_geometry(),
    E = data_map %>% sf::st_drop_geometry() %>% pull(E),
      control.predictor = list(compute = TRUE, link = 1),
      control.compute   = list(dic = TRUE, cpo = TRUE,config = T)
    ),
  silent = TRUE)
 
   y_hat       <- res_null$summary.fitted.values$mean
   resid     <- data_map %>% sf::st_drop_geometry() %>% pull(pharm_count) - y_hat
   
moran_ps[i]= spdep::moran.mc(
      resid,
      listw       = adjacency_list$nb_list,
      nsim        = 999,
      alternative = "two.sided"
    )$p.value

}
)


local_m <- localmoran(
  x     = data_map$pharm_count,
  listw = adjacency_list$nb_list,
  zero.policy = TRUE
)

data_map %>% 
bind_cols(as_tibble(local_m)) %>%
  rename(
    Ii       = `Ii`,
    E_Ii     = `E.Ii`,
    Var_Ii   = `Var.Ii`,
    Z_Ii     = `Z.Ii`,
    local_p  = `Pr(z != E(Ii))`
  ) %>% 
  mutate(
    pharm_std = as.numeric(scale(pharm_count)),
    lag_pharm = lag.listw(adjacency_list$nb_list, pharm_count, zero.policy = TRUE),
    lag_std   = as.numeric(scale(lag_pharm)),
    quadrant  = case_when(
      pharm_std > 0 & lag_std > 0 ~ "High–High",
      pharm_std < 0 & lag_std < 0 ~ "Low–Low",
      pharm_std > 0 & lag_std < 0 ~ "High–Low",
      pharm_std < 0 & lag_std > 0 ~ "Low–High",
      TRUE                         ~ NA_character_
    ),
    quadrant = if_else(local_p < 0.05, quadrant, "Not significant")
  ) %>% 
  ggplot() +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = quadrant), color = NA) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "High–High"       = "#B2182B",
      "Low–Low"         = "#2166AC",
      "High–Low"        = "#F4A582",
      "Low–High"        = "#92C5DE",
      "Not significant" = "grey80"
    ),
    na.value = "grey80",
    drop = FALSE
  ) +
  labs(
    fill = "Local Moran’s I\ngroups",
    title = "Local Moran’s I clusters of pharmacy counts",
    subtitle = "Significance at p < 0.05"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank()
  )

```

### Catchments

We calculate the catchments of all census tracts in both our sample population NYC counties and Westchester and Nassau counties. The reason being is that populations outside of NYC counties that share land can ultimately impact the population weight applied to pharmacies within NYC.

::: panel-tabset
#### Walking

-   [x] 96% coverage in mins while walking

-   [x] 5 min catchment while walking

-   [x] 10 min catchment while walking

-   [x] 15 min catchment while walking

```{r coverage w}

# pharm_time_cen_walk = read_csv("./data/R_walk.csv") %>% 
#   filter(county.x %in% c(36005,
#                  36047,
#                  36061,
#                  36081,
```

```{r Isochrone w}

iso_results_w <- vector("list", nrow(map_only))

# Loop through each row in map_only
for (i in seq_len(nrow(map_only))) {
  # Extract the longitude and latitude for the current row
  lon <- map_only$longitude[i]
  lat <- map_only$latitude[i]
  
  # Try to compute the isochrone; handle errors gracefully
  result <- try(
    osrm::osrmIsochrone(
      loc = c(lon, lat),
      breaks = seq(0, 2, by = 2),
      osrm.server = "http://127.0.0.1:5002/",
      osrm.profile = "foot"
    ),
    silent = TRUE # Suppress error messages
  )
  
  # Check if the result is an error; store NULL if it failed
  if (inherits(result, "try-error")) {
    iso_results_w[[i]] <- NULL
  } else {
    iso_results_w[[i]] <- result
  }
}

# Add the isochrone results as a new column to the map_only dataset
map_only_w = map_only
map_only_w$iso <- iso_results_w

isochrones_walk_2 <- map_only_w 


walk_isochrones_2 = map_only %>% 
    mutate(
  
    iso = purrr::map2(longitude,latitude ,
                   ~osrm::osrmIsochrone(loc = c(.x, .y),
                                       breaks = seq(0,2, by = 2), 
                                     osrm.server = "http://127.0.0.1:5002/",
                                     osrm.profile = "foot")
                   )

) 

isochrones_walk_2 = walk_isochrones_2 %>% 
  # replacing CT polygons with isocrhones 
  #sf::st_drop_geometry() %>% 
 # unnest(iso) %>% 
 # sf::st_set_geometry("geometry") %>% 
  st_transform(crs = 2263) 

 isochrones_walk_5 = map_only %>% 
     mutate(
    
    iso = purrr::map2(longitude,latitude ,
                   ~osrm::osrmIsochrone(loc = c(.x, .y),
                                        # using 10 min walking time 
                                       breaks = seq(0,5, by = 5),
                                     osrm.server = "http://127.0.0.1:5002/",
                                     osrm.profile = "foot")
                   )

) 
  
   isochrones_walk_5 = isochrones_walk_5 %>% 
  # replacing CT polygons with isocrhones 
  sf::st_drop_geometry() %>% 
  unnest(iso) %>% 
  sf::st_set_geometry("geometry") %>% 
  st_transform(crs = 4326)
   
   isochrones_walk_10 = map_only[-1366,] %>% 
     mutate(
    
    iso = purrr::map2(longitude,latitude ,
                   ~osrm::osrmIsochrone(loc = c(.x, .y),
                                        # using 10 min walking time 
                                       breaks = seq(0,10, by = 10),
                                     osrm.server = "http://127.0.0.1:5002/",
                                     osrm.profile = "foot")
                   )

) 
  
   isochrones_walk_10 = isochrones_walk_10 %>% 
  # replacing CT polygons with isocrhones 
  sf::st_drop_geometry() %>% 
  unnest(iso) %>% 
  sf::st_set_geometry("geometry") %>% 
  st_transform(crs = 4326)
   
   isochrones_walk_15 = map_only[c(-793,-794),] %>% 
     mutate(
    
    iso = purrr::map2(longitude,latitude ,
                   ~osrm::osrmIsochrone(loc = c(.x, .y),
                                        # using 10 min walking time 
                                       breaks = seq(0,15, by = 15),
                                     osrm.server = "http://127.0.0.1:5002/",
                                     osrm.profile = "foot")
                   )

) 
  
   isochrones_walk_15 = isochrones_walk_15 %>% 
  # replacing CT polygons with isocrhones 
  sf::st_drop_geometry() %>% 
  unnest(iso) %>% 
  sf::st_set_geometry("geometry") %>% 
  st_transform(crs = 4326)
```

```{r Isocrhones c}

# It seems that for some census tracts the catchments are not able to close, causing the routing machine to break. Therefore some census tracts are removed, those that do not have a complete catchment. 

# Example error: Error in `mutate()`:
# ℹ In argument: `iso = purrr::map2(...)`.
# Caused by error in `purrr::map2()`:
# ℹ In index: 543.
# Caused by error in `CPL_transform()`:
# ! CHAR() can only be applied to a 'CHARSXP', not a 'NULL'

isochrones_2_car_1_93 = map_only[1:93,] %>% 
    mutate(
  
    iso = purrr::map2(longitude,latitude ,
                   ~osrm::osrmIsochrone(loc = c(.x, .y),
                                       breaks = seq(0,2, by = 2), 
                                     osrm.server = "http://127.0.0.1:5001/",
                                     osrm.profile = "car")
                   )

) %>% 
  st_transform(crs = 2263) 

isochrones_2_car_95_1594 = map_only[1:93,] %>% 
    mutate(
  
    iso = purrr::map2(longitude,latitude ,
                   ~osrm::osrmIsochrone(loc = c(.x, .y),
                                       breaks = seq(0,2, by = 2), 
                                     osrm.server = "http://127.0.0.1:5001/",
                                     osrm.profile = "car")
                   )

) %>% 
  st_transform(crs = 2263) 

safe_osrmIsochrone <- purrr::possibly(
  ~osrm::osrmIsochrone(
    loc = c(.x, .y),
    breaks = seq(0, 2, by = 2),
    osrm.server = "http://127.0.0.1:5001/",
    osrm.profile = "car"
  ),
  otherwise = NULL # Return NULL if an error occurs
)

isochrones_2_car <- map_only %>%
  mutate(
    iso = purrr::map2(longitude, latitude, safe_osrmIsochrone) # Apply safe function
    ) %>%
  filter(!purrr::map_lgl(iso, is.null)) %>% # Remove rows with NULL isochrones
  mutate( iso = purrr::map(iso, ~if (!is.null(.x)) st_as_sf(.x) else NULL) # Convert to sf objects
          ) %>%
  st_transform(crs = 2263)

iso_results <- vector("list", nrow(map_only))

# Loop through each row in map_only
for (i in seq_len(nrow(map_only))) {
  # Extract the longitude and latitude for the current row
  lon <- map_only$longitude[i]
  lat <- map_only$latitude[i]
  
  # Try to compute the isochrone; handle errors gracefully
  result <- try(
    osrm::osrmIsochrone(
      loc = c(lon, lat),
      breaks = seq(0, 2, by = 2),
      osrm.server = "http://127.0.0.1:5001/",
      osrm.profile = "car"
    ),
    silent = TRUE # Suppress error messages
  )
  
  # Check if the result is an error; store NULL if it failed
  if (inherits(result, "try-error")) {
    iso_results[[i]] <- NULL
  } else {
    iso_results[[i]] <- result
  }
}

# Add the isochrone results as a new column to the map_only dataset
map_only$iso <- iso_results

isochrones_2_car <- map_only %>%
 # mutate(
  #  iso = purrr::map(iso, ~if (!is.null(.x)) st_as_sf(.x) else NULL)) %>%
  #filter(!purrr::map_lgl(iso, is.null)) %>% # Keep rows with valid isochrones
  st_transform(crs = 2263) 

str(isochrones_2_car)

isochrones_2_car %>% sf::st_is_valid() %>% table()
```

```{r}
# Otherwise using the following functions do work, because the catchments are able to be complete.

car_5 = isochrones_func(map_only,"latitude","longitude",5, "http://127.0.0.1:5001/", "car")
car_10 = isochrones_func(map_only,"latitude","longitude",10, "http://127.0.0.1:5001/", "car")
car_15 = isochrones_func(map_only,"latitude","longitude",15, "http://127.0.0.1:5001/", "car")

```

It is important to consider the proper coordinate reference system (CRS) when projecting points onto a map, and when measuring proximities. The reason is that a particular CRS for New York City may provide more accurate measurements given that those measurements can be obscured by the curvature of the Earth over large distances. CRS 2263 is specific for the New York Area and provides more accurate proximity measures than the other CRS's [ref](https://nycitymap.wordpress.com/2016/09/13/nyc-projected/).

```{r Projections c}
                 36085
           )), fill = "blue", alpha = 0.3) +
  coord_sf(xlim = c(-74.3,-73.6), ylim = c(40.5,40.9),crs = 4263)
```

```{r nearest car}
  filter(county.x %in% c(36005,

  group_by(GEOID) %>%

# 56.2 sex = 0.94 mins
nearest_pharm_car_1mile %>% 
   filter(county.x %in% c(36005,
                 36047,
                 36061,
                 36081,
                 36085
           )) %>% 
  group_by(GEOID) %>% 
  arrange(duration) %>% 
  mutate(order = row_number()) %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
filter(order == 1) %>% 
  unique() %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  arrange(duration) %>% 
  mutate(cumulative_percent = cumsum(population) / sum(population),
         quant_96 = quantile(duration/60, 0.96)) %>% 
  unique() %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = duration/60, y = cumulative_percent ))+
  geom_line() +
  #geom_vline(xintercept = 2.214267)+
  labs(x = "Duration In Minutes", y = "Cumulative Percent")

   filter(county.x %in% c(36005,
                 36047,
                 36061,
                 36081,
                 36085
           )) %>% 
  group_by(GEOID) %>% 
  arrange(duration) %>% 
  mutate(order = row_number()) %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
filter(order == 1) %>% 
  unique() %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  arrange(duration) %>% 
  mutate(cumulative_percent = cumsum(population) / sum(population),
         quant_96 = quantile(duration/60, 0.96)) %>% 
  unique() %>% 
pull(duration) %>% 
  quantile(0.96)

# 2 min for 96%

```

Here we assess the border effect on access to pharmacy using 2 min catchments. There are 2743 census tracts that remain after the exclusion and inclusion criteria. Only 1849 receive an access score when considering pharmacies beyond the border, and 1502 when only considering pharmacies within NYC. This suggests that a difference of 347 census tracts do not recieve a score simply because their closest pharmacies are outside of the New York City boundary. Nonetheless, the distribution of the access score is virtually the same.

```{r driving interactice maps}
    highlightOptions = highlightOptions(
      weight = 2,
      color = "blue",

      bringToFront = TRUE

    label = map_labels,
    labelOptions = labelOptions(
      style = list("font-weight" = "bold", "color" = "black"),
      textsize = "12px",
      direction = "auto"
    )
  ) %>% 
  addLegend(
    pal = map_pop_pal,
```

```{r 96% Coverage d}
#| eval: false
distance_car = d_1 %>% 
   select(query, within_isochrone) %>%
  # duplicating the number of pharmacies by the number of isochrones they reside in
  unnest(within_isochrone) %>%
  sf::st_drop_geometry() %>%
  # connecing isochrone points data set to shore file to get the CT geoid they belong to
  left_join(isochrones %>%
              sf::st_drop_geometry() %>%
              # creating ids of isochrones
              mutate(within_isochrone = row_number(),
                     geoid = GEOID),
            by = "within_isochrone" ) %>%
  sf::st_drop_geometry() %>%
  left_join(
    pharm_geocode_unique,
    by = "query"
  ) %>%
  distance_url(lat1 = "latitude",lat2 = "location.lat", lng1 = "longitude", lng2 = "location.lng", profile = "car", server = "http://127.0.0.1:5001/" )



pharm_dis_cen_map = tigris::geo_join(
  map_only %>% 
    filter(county.x %in% c(36005,
                 36047,
                 36061,
                 36081,
                 36085)),
  distance_car %>% 
    select(geoid.x, population, distance) %>% 
    mutate(geoid.x = as.character(geoid.x)) %>% 
    group_by(geoid.x) %>% 
    filter(distance == min(distance,na.rm = T)) %>% 
    unique() %>% 
    ungroup(),
  by_sp = "GEOID",
  by_df = "geoid.x",
  how = "inner"
)

  ggplot()+
  geom_sf(aes(fill = log(distance)))

pharm_dis_cen_map %>% 
  sf::st_drop_geometry() %>% 
  pull(distance) %>% 
  quantile(0.96)
```

:::

# LWC Simulation

```{r}

# gathering pharmacies within 5 min walking
pnts_walk_5 %>%
    select(query, within_isochrone) %>% # withing_isochrone is the row id of the census tract.
    # duplicating the number of pharmacies by the number of isochrones they reside in
    unnest(within_isochrone) %>%# pull(within_isochrone)
    sf::st_drop_geometry() %>%
    # connecing isochrone points data set to shore file to get the CT geoid they belong to
    left_join(isochrones_walk_5 %>%
                sf::st_drop_geometry() %>%
                select(GEOID) %>% # GEOID is the census tract id 
                # creating ids of isochrones
                mutate(within_isochrone = row_number()), # making a copy of census tract geoid
              by = "within_isochrone" ) %>%
    sf::st_drop_geometry() %>%
  mutate(within_iso_w = 1) %>% # prepaing dataset for next merge to keep track of census tracts mode of transportation proximity to pharmacy
  rename(within_isochrone_w = within_isochrone) %>% # prepaing dataset for next merge to keep track of census tracts mode of transportation proximity to pharmacy
  

  # merging walking 5 min isochrones with 5 min driving isochrones
    
    full_join(
      
      pnts_car_5 %>%
        select(query, within_isochrone) %>% 
        unnest(within_isochrone) %>%
        sf::st_drop_geometry() %>%
        left_join(car_5 %>%
                    sf::st_drop_geometry() %>%
                    select(GEOID) %>% 
                    mutate(within_isochrone = row_number()),
                  by = "within_isochrone" ) %>%
        sf::st_drop_geometry() %>% 
        mutate(within_iso_c = 1) %>% 
        rename(within_isochrone_c = within_isochrone) %>% 
        select(query, within_iso_c, within_isochrone_c),
       by = c("query", "within_isochrone_w" = "within_isochrone_c")
    )  %>% 
  left_join( # adds pharmacy coordinates to dataset for later plotting 
      pharm_geocode_unique,
      by = "query"
    ) %>% 
  mutate_at(vars(within_iso_c, within_iso_w),~replace_na(.x,0))  %>%
 # pull(within_iso_c) %>% table() # there are 7576/212079 driving, 181042/38613 walking
  
  left_join(data1 %>% 
              rename("GEOID" = "geoid")  , # joining by census tract id
            by = "GEOID") %>% 
  mutate(prop_drive = estimate_car_truck_van/estimate_total_trans, # creating proportions of those who drive or not drive to work
         prop_non_drive = 1-prop_drive) %>% 
  group_by(query) %>% 
  mutate(R = (population*(prop_drive + prop_non_drive))^(-1)) %>% 
    ungroup() %>%
    # grouping by CT to now add the R_j's that make up the access score A_i for each CT
    group_by(GEOID) %>%
    # A reads as the pharmacy rate per 10000 populous
    summarize(A = sum(R, na.rm = T)*10000
    ) %>%
    ungroup()

data1 %>% 
  select(estimate_car_truck_van,estimate_total_trans,moe_total_trans,moe_car_truck_van) %>% 
  mutate(pi = (estimate_car_truck_van/estimate_total_trans) %>% round(.,2))
```

The goal of simulation is to estimate a linearly weighted combination of access scores with measures of uncertainty. Only the driving and walking estimates are used because the simulation requires that the weights add up to 100. Also, note that the estimates are only given with margins of error. In the ACS documentation, the moe is derived using 90% confidence intervals and the standard error is the standard deviation. That means, the standard deviation is equal to the moe/1.645.

```{r}
                        iso_c = car_10,
                        iso_w = isochrones_walk_10,
n_sims = 100) %>% 
  rename("geoid" = "GEOID", "lwc_A10"= "median_A","lwc_A10_0.025" ="q0.025","lwc_A10_0.975"="q0.975", "p_A10" = "mean_p"),
    by = "geoid") %>% 
  full_join(
lwc_access(data = data1 %>% rename("query" = "address1"), 
           pnts_c = pnts_car_15 %>% rename("query" = "address1"), 
                        pnts_w = pnts_walk_15 %>% rename("query" = "address1"),
                        iso_c = car_15,
                        iso_w = isochrones_walk_15, 
           n_sims = 100) %>%
  rename("geoid" = "GEOID", "lwc_A15"= "median_A","lwc_A15_0.025" ="q0.025","lwc_A15_0.975"="q0.975", "p_A15" = "mean_p"),
    by = "geoid") 



   # adding rx pharmacy -------------
aim1_data_final = aim1_data_final %>% 
  full_join(
      lwc_access(
        data = data1, 
             pnts_c = pnts_car_5 %>% rename("query" = "name"),
             pnts_w = pnts_walk_5 %>% rename("query" = "name"),
             iso_c = car_5,
             iso_w = isochrones_walk_5,
             n_sims = 2) %>% 
        rename("geoid" = "GEOID", "lwc_A5"= "median_A","lwc_A5_0.025" ="q0.025","lwc_A5_0.975"="q0.975", "p_A5" = "mean_p"),
      by = "geoid"
      )



aim1_data %>%
  mutate_at(vars(w_A5:d_A1_nyc), ~replace_na(.,0)) %>% 
   mutate_at(vars(lwc_A2:lwc_A15_0.975), ~replace_na(.,0)) %>% 
  full_join(
    data1,
    by = "geoid"
  ) %>% 
  filter(county.x %in% c(36005,36047,36061,36081,36085 )) %>% 
  select(geoid, w_A5:w_A2,lwc_A2:p_A15, c_A5:c_A2) %>% 
  pivot_longer(
    w_A5:c_A2,
    names_to = "score_name",
    values_to = "scores"
    
  ) %>% 
  separate(score_name, into = c("type","time"), sep = "_") %>% 
  filter(scores<30) %>% 
  mutate(type = forcats::fct_relevel(type, "w","c","lwc"),
         scores = if_else(type == "p",scores*10, scores)) %>% 
  ggplot()+
  geom_boxplot(aes(x = type, y = scores, fill = type))+
    facet_wrap(~time)+
    scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set3")
```

## Dep/Ind X

```{r}
# Load the package for truncated normal draws
library(truncnorm)

set.seed(123)
n <- 100

### CASE 1: Independent X and Y drawn from Normal distributions

# Generate X ~ N(10, 2^2)
X_ind <- rnorm(n, mean = 10, sd = 2)
# Generate Y ~ N(20, 3^2)
Y_ind <- rnorm(n, mean = 20, sd = 3)
# Force Y to be positive (if any draws are <= 0, replace them with 20)
Y_ind <- ifelse(Y_ind <= 0, 20, Y_ind)

# Compute the ratio
ratio_ind <- X_ind / Y_ind

# Summary for independent ratio:
summary(ratio_ind)
hist(ratio_ind, main = "Histogram of X/Y (Independent)", xlab = "X/Y", col = "lightblue")


### CASE 2: Dependent simulation with truncated normals

# Generate Y ~ TN(a = 0, b = Inf, mean = 20, sd = 3)
Y_trunc <- rtruncnorm(n, a = 0, b = Inf, mean = 20, sd = 3)

# Set the scaling parameters for X|Y
m <- 0.5   # desired proportion (mean of X|Y is m * Y)
s <- 0.1   # relative standard deviation

# For each drawn Y, generate X from a truncated normal with:
# lower bound = 0, upper bound = Y, mean = m*Y, sd = s*Y.
# Note: rtruncnorm does not vectorize the 'b' parameter, so we use sapply.
X_cond <- sapply(Y_trunc, function(y) {
  rtruncnorm(n = 1, a = 0, b = y, mean = m * y, sd = s * y)
})

# Compute the ratio for the dependent case
ratio_cond <- X_cond / Y_trunc

# Summary for dependent ratio:
summary(ratio_cond)
hist(ratio_cond, main = "Histogram of X/Y (Dependent)", xlab = "X/Y", col = "lightgreen")


### Compare the two distributions (boxplot, for example)
boxplot(ratio_ind, ratio_cond, names = c("Independent", "Dependent"),
        main = "Comparison of X/Y distributions", col = c("lightblue", "lightgreen"))

# Optionally, compare summary statistics side by side:
ind_stats <- c(mean = mean(ratio_ind), median = median(ratio_ind),
               quantile(ratio_ind, 0.025), quantile(ratio_ind, 0.975))
cond_stats <- c(mean = mean(ratio_cond), median = median(ratio_cond),
                quantile(ratio_cond, 0.025), quantile(ratio_cond, 0.975))
stats_df <- rbind(Independent = ind_stats, Dependent = cond_stats)
print(stats_df)

```

## Hierarchical/Prop

```{r}


set.seed(123)
n <- 5  # number of tracts
n_rep <- 100  # number of simulation replications

# Data generating process for each tract:
generate_true <- function() {
  
  # Generate margins of error
  MOE_Y <- 50 #rbinom(n = 1,10,prob = 0.8)*1.645
  MOE_X <- 25 #rbinom(n = 1,10,prob = 0.2)*1.645
  # Generate new mean counts, Y = total who work, X = those who drive
  Y <-  rtruncnorm(100, a = 1, mean = 100, sd = MOE_Y/1.645)
  hat_y = mean(Y)
  X <- rtruncnorm(100, a = 0, b = Y, mean = 0.5 * Y, sd = MOE_X/1.645)
  hat_x = mean(X)
  p<- mean(X/Y)
  
  list(Y = hat_y, MOE_Y = MOE_Y, X = hat_x, MOE_X = MOE_X, p = p)
}

# Generate one dataset of n tracts
true_data <- replicate(n, generate_true(), simplify = FALSE)
Y_true <- sapply(true_data, `[[`, "Y")
MOE_Y_true <- sapply(true_data, `[[`, "MOE_Y")
X_true <- sapply(true_data, `[[`, "X")
MOE_X_true <- sapply(true_data, `[[`, "MOE_X")
p_true <- sapply(true_data, `[[`, "p")



# Direct method:
simulate_direct <- function(X, Y, MOE_X, MOE_Y) {
  se_p <- (1 / Y) * sqrt((MOE_X / 1.645)^2 + ((X / Y)^2) * (MOE_Y / 1.645)^2)
  rtruncnorm(1, a = 0, b = 1, mean = X / Y, sd = se_p)
}

# Hierarchical X only method:
simulate_hierarchical_x <- function(X, Y, MOE_X, MOE_Y) {
  X_star <- rtruncnorm(1, a = 0, b = Y, mean = X, sd = MOE_X/1.645)
  X_star / Y
}

# Hierarchical method:
simulate_hierarchical <- function(X, Y, MOE_X, MOE_Y) {
  # Simulate Y* 
  Y_star <- rtruncnorm(1, a = 0, mean = Y, sd = MOE_Y/1.645)
  # Simulate X* 
  X_star <- rtruncnorm(1, a = 0, b = Y_star, mean = X, sd = MOE_X/1.645)
  X_star / Y_star
}

simulations <- data.frame(
  tract = integer(),
  method = character(),
  rep = integer(),
  p_sim = numeric(),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

results <- data.frame(
  method = character(), 
  tract = integer(), 
  error = numeric(), 
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )

for (i in 1:n) {
  for (rep in 1:n_rep) {
    p_d <- simulate_direct(X_true[i], Y_true[i], MOE_X_true[i], MOE_Y_true[i])
    p_h_x <- simulate_hierarchical_x(X_true[i], Y_true[i], MOE_X_true[i], MOE_Y_true[i])
    p_h <- simulate_hierarchical(X_true[i], Y_true[i], MOE_X_true[i], MOE_Y_true[i])
    
    simulations <- rbind(simulations,
                         data.frame(tract = i, method = "direct", rep = rep, p_sim = p_d),
                         data.frame(tract = i, method = "hierarchical_x", rep = rep, p_sim = p_h_x),
                         data.frame(tract = i, method = "hierarchical", rep = rep, p_sim = p_h))
    
    results <- rbind(results,
                     data.frame(method = "direct", tract = i, error = abs(p_true[i] - p_d)),
                     data.frame(method = "hierarchical_x", tract = i, error = abs(p_true[i] - p_h_x)),
                     data.frame(method = "hierarchical", tract = i, error = abs(p_true[i] - p_h)))
  }
}

summary_results <- results %>%
  mutate(method = forcats::fct_relevel(method, "direct", "hierarchical_x")) %>% 
  group_by(method) %>%
  summarize(mean_error = mean(error), mse = mean(error^2))

print(summary_results)

### Compute 95% intervals and interval widths for each tract and method
coverage_summary <- simulations %>% 
  mutate(method = forcats::fct_relevel(method, "direct", "hierarchical_x")) %>% 
  group_by(tract, method) %>%
  summarize(
    lower = quantile(p_sim, 0.025, na.rm = TRUE),
    upper = quantile(p_sim, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE),
    width = upper - lower,
    coverage = if_else(p_true[tract[1]] >= lower & p_true[tract[1]] <= upper, 1, 0),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) 

### Plot interval widths of 95% confidence intervals by method
ggplot(coverage_summary, aes(x = method, y = width, fill = method)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "95% Interval Widths by Method", x = "Method", y = "Interval Width")


```

## Using Simstudy

```{r}


# Load required packages
library(simstudy)
library(truncnorm)

set.seed(123)
n <- 5         # number of tracts
n_rep <- 1000  # number of simulation replications per tract

### STEP 1. Generate a “true” dataset using simstudy

# Define a data definition.
# Here we generate two raw MOE values (from a binomial) and then convert them to MOE's.
def <- defData(varname = "raw_MOE_Y", dist = "binomial", formula = "10,0.8", id = "id")
def <- defData(def, varname = "MOE_Y", dist = "binomial", formula = "raw_MOE_Y * 1.645")
def <- defData(def, varname = "raw_MOE_X", dist = "binomial", formula = "10,0.2")
def <- defData(def, varname = "MOE_X", dist = "nonrandom", formula = "raw_MOE_X * 1.645")

# Generate n tracts with the above definitions
true_dt <- genData(n, def)

### STEP 2. Create the “true” counts for Y and X

# For each tract, we generate:
#   Y ~ truncated normal with lower bound 0, mean = 100, and sd = MOE_Y/1.645.
#   X ~ truncated normal with lower bound 0, upper bound = Y, mean = 0.5 * Y, and sd = MOE_X/1.645.
true_dt <- true_dt %>%
  mutate(
    Y = sapply(MOE_Y, function(m) rtruncnorm(1, a = 0, mean = 100, sd = m/1.645)),
    Y = ifelse(Y == 0, 1, Y),
    X = mapply(function(y, moeX) rtruncnorm(1, a = 0, b = y, mean = 0.5 * y, sd = moeX/1.645),
               Y, MOE_X),
    p = X / Y
  )

# Extract true values (vectors of length n)
Y_true      <- true_dt$Y
MOE_Y_true  <- true_dt$MOE_Y
X_true      <- true_dt$X
MOE_X_true  <- true_dt$MOE_X
p_true      <- true_dt$p

### STEP 3. Define simulation functions for new proportions

# Direct method: simulate p directly from a truncated normal on [0,1]
simulate_direct <- function(X, Y, MOE_X, MOE_Y) {
  se_p <- (1 / Y) * sqrt((MOE_X / 1.645)^2 + ((X / Y)^2) * (MOE_Y / 1.645)^2)
  rtruncnorm(1, a = 0, b = 1, mean = X / Y, sd = se_p)
}

# Hierarchical method: first simulate a new total Y*, then simulate X* given Y*
simulate_hierarchical <- function(X, Y, MOE_X, MOE_Y) {
  # Simulate a new total from the ACS distribution:
  Y_star <- rtruncnorm(1, a = 0, mean = Y, sd = MOE_Y / 1.645)
  # Simulate a new driver count given Y*.
  # Note: We now use X as the mean (with MOE_X) and restrict X* to [0, Y_star].
  X_star <- rtruncnorm(1, a = 0, b = Y_star, mean = X, sd = MOE_X / 1.645)
  X_star / Y_star
}

### STEP 4. Run simulation replications for each tract & method

simulations <- data.frame(
  tract = integer(),
  method = character(),
  rep = integer(),
  p_sim = numeric(),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

for (i in 1:n) {
  for (rep in 1:n_rep) {
    p_d <- simulate_direct(X_true[i], Y_true[i], MOE_X_true[i], MOE_Y_true[i])
    p_h <- simulate_hierarchical(X_true[i], Y_true[i], MOE_X_true[i], MOE_Y_true[i])
    simulations <- rbind(simulations,
                         data.frame(tract = i, method = "direct", rep = rep, p_sim = p_d),
                         data.frame(tract = i, method = "hierarchical", rep = rep, p_sim = p_h))
  }
}

### STEP 5. Compute 95% Credible Intervals, Interval Widths, and Coverage

coverage_summary <- simulations %>%
  group_by(tract, method) %>%
  summarize(
    lower = quantile(p_sim, 0.025, na.rm = TRUE),
    upper = quantile(p_sim, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE),
    width = upper - lower,
    coverage = if_else(p_true[tract[1]] >= lower & p_true[tract[1]] <= upper, 1, 0),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

summary_stats <- coverage_summary %>%
  group_by(method) %>%
  summarize(
    median_width = median(width),
    coverage_prob = median(coverage),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

print(summary_stats)

### STEP 6. Plot Interval Widths by Method

ggplot(coverage_summary, aes(x = method, y = width, fill = method)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "95% Interval Widths by Method", x = "Method", y = "Interval Width")



```

```{r}
sim_acs = function(GEOID, n_sims, acs_car, 
                   car_moe, acs_tot, 
                   pnts_c,pnts_w,iso_c,iso_w){
  

  
  if(is.na(acs_car)){
    
    sim = tibble(
    sim_car = NA) %>% 
    mutate(
      lwc = NA
    )
    return(sim)
  }else{
    
      iso = join_iso(
    pnts_c = pnts_c,
    pnts_w = pnts_w,
    iso_c = iso_c,
    iso_w = iso_w
  ) %>% 
    filter(GEOID == GEOID)
    
  car_sd = car_moe/1.645
  
  sim_car =  rnorm(n_sims,acs_car, car_sd)

  sim = tibble(
    prop_drive = sim_car/acs_tot,
    prop_non_drive = 1-prop_drive) %>% 
    mutate(
      lwc = (prop_drive*access_c + sim_walk*access_w)/(2*sim_tot)
    )
  
  return(sim)
  }
  
  sim
  
}



set.seed(123)

  select(-c(estimate_total_trans,name,moe_total_trans)) %>% 
  #left_join(acs_raw, by = "geoid") %>% 
  mutate(output = pmap(list(estimate_car_truck_van, 
                            moe_car_truck_van, 
                            estimate_walked, 
                            moe_walked, 
                            w_A2,
                            c_A2),
                       ~sim_acs(1000, ..1,..2,..3,..4,..5,..6)
                       
                       ) 
         ) %>% 
  unnest(cols = output) %>% 
  group_by(geoid) %>% 
  summarize(
    mean = mean(lwc, na.rm= T),
    median = median(lwc, na.rm= T),
    q0.025 = quantile(lwc, 0.025, na.rm= T),
    q0.975 = quantile(lwc, 0.975, na.rm= T)
    
  )
```

```{r}
                 36047,

access_walk = read_rds("./data/access_walk_time.rds")
access_car = read_rds("./data/access_car_time.rds")
access_d = read_rds("./data/access_distance.rds")

map_access_data <- function(map_shp, data, access){

  data = data %>%
    select(geoid,pharm_count,pharm_den, population, ind, SIR, E) %>%
    unique() %>%
    left_join(access, by = "geoid")

  tigris::geo_join(
    map_shp,
    data ,
    by_sp = "GEOID",
    by_df = "geoid",
    how = "inner") %>%
    filter(county %in% c(36005,
                         36047,
                         36061,
                         36081,
                         36085 ))

}

plot_access_map <- function(data, var_name) {
  # Dynamically rename and calculate quantiles
  data <- data %>%
    rename(A_g = {{ var_name }}) %>%
    mutate(Ag_5_q = case_when(
   #   A_g == 0 ~ "0",
      A_g > 0 & A_g <= quantile(A_g, na.rm = TRUE)[2] ~ "1",
      A_g > quantile(A_g, na.rm = TRUE)[2] & A_g <= quantile(A_g, na.rm = TRUE)[3] ~ "2",
      A_g > quantile(A_g, na.rm = TRUE)[3] & A_g <= quantile(A_g, na.rm = TRUE)[4] ~ "3",
      A_g > quantile(A_g, na.rm = TRUE)[4] ~ "4"
    ))

  # Plot the map
  ggplot(data) +
    geom_sf(aes(fill = Ag_5_q)) +
    scale_fill_viridis_d(
      option = "B",
      name = as.character(substitute(var_name)),  # Dynamic legend name
      na.value = "gray60"  # NA values in gray
    )
}
```

### uncertainty

```{r}

geoids_uncertain2 = aim1_data %>%
  mutate_at(vars(w_A5:d_A1_nyc), ~replace_na(., 0)) %>%

geoids_uncertain5 = aim1_data %>%
  mutate_at(vars(w_A5:d_A1_nyc), ~replace_na(., 0)) %>% 
  mutate_at(vars(lwc_A2:lwc_A15_0.975), ~replace_na(., 0)) %>% 
  full_join(data1, by = "geoid") %>% 
  mutate(row = row_number()) %>% 
  filter(county.x %in% c(36005, 36047, 36061, 36081, 36085)#,
        # row %in% sample(2000, 200)
         ) %>% 
 # select(geoid, w_A5:w_A2, lwc_A2:lwc_A15_0.975, c_A5:c_A2, row) %>% 
    mutate(ind = if_else(lwc_A5_0.975-lwc_A5 > 1 & lwc_A5-lwc_A5_0.025>1, 1,0)) %>% 
    filter(ind == 1) %>% 
    select(geoid#,population.x
           ) #%>% 
  summarize(median = median(population.x, rm.na= T))

geoids_uncertain10 = aim1_data %>%
  mutate_at(vars(w_A5:d_A1_nyc), ~replace_na(., 0)) %>% 
  mutate_at(vars(lwc_A2:lwc_A15_0.975), ~replace_na(., 0)) %>% 
  full_join(data1, by = "geoid") %>% 
  mutate(row = row_number()) %>% 
  filter(county.x %in% c(36005, 36047, 36061, 36081, 36085)#,
        # row %in% sample(2000, 200)
         ) %>% 
  select(geoid, w_A5:w_A2, lwc_A2:lwc_A15_0.975, c_A5:c_A2, row) %>% 
    mutate(ind = if_else(lwc_A10_0.975-lwc_A10 > 1 & lwc_A10-lwc_A10_0.025>1, 1,0)) %>% 
    filter(ind == 1) %>% 
    select(geoid)

geoids_uncertain15 = aim1_data %>%
  mutate_at(vars(w_A5:d_A1_nyc), ~replace_na(., 0)) %>% 
  mutate_at(vars(lwc_A2:lwc_A15_0.975), ~replace_na(., 0)) %>% 
  full_join(data1, by = "geoid") %>% 
  mutate(row = row_number()) %>% 
  filter(county.x %in% c(36005, 36047, 36061, 36081, 36085)#,
        # row %in% sample(2000, 200)
         ) %>% 
  select(geoid, w_A5:w_A2, lwc_A2:lwc_A15_0.975, c_A5:c_A2, row) %>% 
    mutate(ind = if_else(lwc_A15_0.975-lwc_A15 > 1 & lwc_A15-lwc_A15_0.025>1, 1,0)) %>% 
    filter(ind == 1) %>% 
    select(geoid)


aim1_map_data %>%
  mutate_at(vars(w_A5:d_A1_nyc), ~replace_na(.,0)) %>% 
   mutate_at(vars(lwc_A2:lwc_A15_0.975), ~replace_na(.,0)) %>% 
  left_join(geoids_uncertain2 %>% 
              rename("GEOID" = "geoid") %>% 
              mutate(fill = 1),
            by = "GEOID") %>% 
  mutate(
    fill = if_else(is.na(fill), 0L, 1L),
    fill = factor(fill, levels = c(0, 1),
                  labels = c("CI ≤ 1", "CI > 1"))
  ) %>% 
  ggplot()+
geom_sf(aes(fill = fill), color = NA) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("CI ≤ 1" = "grey80",
               "CI > 1" = "red"),
    name = ""
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    panel.grid = element_blank()
  )
```

### Catchment sensitivity

```{r}

aim1_map_data %>%
  mutate_at(vars(w_A5:d_A1_nyc), ~replace_na(.,0)) %>% 
   mutate_at(vars(lwc_A2:lwc_A15_0.975), ~replace_na(.,0)) %>% 
  filter(c_A2 < quantile(c_A2, 0.95),
         c_A5 < quantile(c_A5, 0.95),
         c_A10 < quantile(c_A10, 0.95),
         c_A15 < quantile(c_A15, 0.95)) %>% 

  ggplot() +
  geom_density(aes(x = c_A2, fill = factor("2 min", levels = c("2 min", "5 min", "10 min", "15 min"))), alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_density(aes(x = c_A5, fill = factor("5 min", levels = c("2 min", "5 min", "10 min", "15 min"))), alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_density(aes(x = c_A10, fill = factor("10 min", levels = c("2 min", "5 min", "10 min", "15 min"))), alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_density(aes(x = c_A15, fill = factor("15 min", levels = c("2 min", "5 min", "10 min", "15 min"))), alpha = 0.3) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(4, "Set2"), 
                    name = "Driving Catchment Size")+
  labs(x = "Number of Pharmacies per 10,000", y = "Proportion")

aim1_map_data %>%
  mutate_at(vars(w_A5:d_A1_nyc), ~replace_na(.,0)) %>% 
   mutate_at(vars(lwc_A2:lwc_A15_0.975), ~replace_na(.,0)) %>% 
  filter(w_A2 < quantile(w_A2, 0.95),
         w_A5 < quantile(w_A5, 0.95),
         w_A10 < quantile(w_A10, 0.95),
         w_A15 < quantile(w_A15, 0.95)) %>% 

  ggplot() +
  geom_density(aes(x = w_A2, fill = factor("2 min", levels = c("2 min", "5 min", "10 min", "15 min"))), alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_density(aes(x = w_A5, fill = factor("5 min", levels = c("2 min", "5 min", "10 min", "15 min"))), alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_density(aes(x = w_A10, fill = factor("10 min", levels = c("2 min", "5 min", "10 min", "15 min"))), alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_density(aes(x = w_A15, fill = factor("15 min", levels = c("2 min", "5 min", "10 min", "15 min"))), alpha = 0.3) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(4, "Set2"), 
                    name = "Walking Catchment Size")+
  labs(x = "Number of Pharmacies per 10,000", y = "Proportion")

aim1_map_data %>%
  mutate_at(vars(w_A5:d_A1_nyc), ~replace_na(.,0)) %>% 
   mutate_at(vars(lwc_A2:lwc_A15_0.975), ~replace_na(.,0)) %>% 
  filter(d_A0.5 < quantile(d_A0.5, 0.95),
         d_A0.61 < quantile(d_A0.61, 0.95),
         d_A1 < quantile(d_A1, 0.95)) %>% 

  ggplot() +
  geom_density(aes(x = d_A0.5, fill = factor("0.5 mi", levels = c("0.5 mi", "0.61 mi", "1 mi"))), alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_density(aes(x = d_A0.61, fill = factor("0.61 mi", levels = c("0.5 mi", "0.61 mi", "1 mi"))), alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_density(aes(x = d_A1, fill = factor("1 mi", levels = c("0.5 mi", "0.61 mi", "1 mi"))), alpha = 0.3) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(3, "Set2"), 
                    name = "Distance (Miles) Catchment Size")+
  labs(x = "Number of Pharmacies per 10,000", y = "Proportion")



aim1_map_data %>%
  mutate_at(vars(w_A5:d_A1_nyc), ~replace_na(., 0)) %>%
  mutate_at(vars(lwc_A2:lwc_A15_0.975), ~replace_na(., 0)) %>%
  sf::st_drop_geometry() %>%
  select(w_A2, w_A5, w_A10, w_A15,
         c_A2, c_A5, c_A10, c_A15,
         d_A0.5, d_A0.61, d_A1) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "score", values_to = "value") %>%
  mutate(
    mode = case_when(
      str_starts(score, "w") ~ "Walking",
      str_starts(score, "c") ~ "Driving",
      str_starts(score, "d") ~ "Distance"
    ),
    threshold = case_when(
      score %in% c("w_A2",  "c_A2")    ~ "2 min",
      score %in% c("w_A5",  "c_A5")    ~ "5 min",
      score %in% c("w_A10", "c_A10")   ~ "10 min",
      score %in% c("w_A15", "c_A15")   ~ "15 min",
      score == "d_A0.5"                ~ "0.5 mi",
      score == "d_A0.61"               ~ "0.61 mi",
      score == "d_A1"                  ~ "1 mi"
    ),
    # order the x-axis within each panel
    threshold = factor(threshold, levels = c("2 min","5 min","10 min","15 min",
                                             "0.5 mi","0.61 mi","1 mi")),
    mode = factor(mode, levels = c("Walking", "Driving", "Distance"))
  ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = threshold, y = value, fill = mode)) +
  geom_boxplot(outliers = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~ mode, scales = "free_x", nrow = 1) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2", guide = "none") +
  labs(
    x = "Catchment Specification",
    y = "Access Score (pharmacies per 10,000)",
    title = "Pharmacy Access Scores by Catchment Specification and Travel Mode"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )
  

```

### Boardereffects

```{r}


aim1_map_data %>%
  mutate_at(vars(w_A5:d_A1_nyc), ~replace_na(.,0)) %>% 
   mutate_at(vars(lwc_A2:lwc_A15_0.975), ~replace_na(.,0)) %>% 
         mutate(diff_score = c_A15_nyc - c_A15,
                border = if_else( diff_score == 0, "Equal", if_else(diff_score >= 1, "Overestimate",if_else(diff_score <= -1 ,"Underestimate","Negligible"))) %>% factor()) %>% 
  ggplot()+
  geom_sf(aes(fill = border))+
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2", name = "Fifteen Minute Border Effect \nWhen Driving")

aim1_map_data %>%
  mutate_at(vars(w_A5:d_A1_nyc), ~replace_na(.,0)) %>% 
   mutate_at(vars(lwc_A2:lwc_A15_0.975), ~replace_na(.,0)) %>% 
         mutate(diff_score = c_A10_nyc - c_A10,
                border = if_else( diff_score == 0, "Equal", if_else(diff_score >= 1, "Overestimate",if_else(diff_score <= -1 ,"Underestimate","Not AP"))) %>% factor()) %>% 
  ggplot()+
  geom_sf(aes(fill = border))+
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2", name = "Ten Minute Border Effect \nWhen Driving")

tableby(~ border_5+border_10+border_15, data = aim1_map_data %>%
         mutate(diff_score_5 = c_A5_nyc - c_A5,
                diff_score_10 = c_A10_nyc - c_A10,
                diff_score_15 = c_A15_nyc - c_A15,
                border_5 = if_else( diff_score_5 == 0, "Equal", if_else(diff_score_5 >= 1, "overestimate",if_else(diff_score_5 <= -1 ,"underestimate","Non-App"))) %>% factor(),
                border_10 = if_else( diff_score_10 == 0, "Equal", if_else(diff_score_10 >= 1, "overestimate",if_else(diff_score_10 <= -1 ,"underestimate","Non-App"))) %>% factor(),
                border_15 = if_else( diff_score_15 == 0, "Equal", if_else(diff_score_15 >= 1, "overestimate",if_else(diff_score_15 <= -1 ,"underestimate","Non-App"))) %>% factor()), control = my_controls) %>% 
  summary()



min_diff <- aim1_map_data %>%
  mutate_at(vars(w_A5:d_A1_nyc), ~replace_na(., 0)) %>%
  mutate_at(vars(lwc_A2:lwc_A15_0.975), ~replace_na(., 0)) %>%
  mutate(diff_score = c_A15_nyc - c_A15) %>%
  pull(diff_score) %>%
  min(na.rm = TRUE)  # will be negative

aim1_map_data %>%
  mutate_at(vars(w_A5:d_A1_nyc), ~replace_na(., 0)) %>%
  mutate_at(vars(lwc_A2:lwc_A15_0.975), ~replace_na(., 0)) %>%
  mutate(diff_score = abs(c_A15_nyc - c_A15)) %>%  # flip to positive for intuitive legend
  ggplot() +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = diff_score), size = 0.05) +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c("white", "white", "#f0c040", "#b2182b"),
    values = scales::rescale(c(0, 1, 2, abs(min_diff))),
    limits = c(0, abs(min_diff)),
    oob    = scales::squish,
    breaks = c(0, 1, floor(abs(min_diff)/2), floor(abs(min_diff))),
    labels = c("0\n(No effect)", "1", 
               as.character(floor(abs(min_diff)/2)), 
               as.character(floor(abs(min_diff)))),
    name   = "Fifteen Minute Border Effect\nWhen Driving\n(pharmacies per 10,000\nunderestimated)",
    na.value = "grey85"
  ) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(legend.position = "right")
```

```{r}
diff_labels <- 
            paste0(
                "Tract: ",
                aim1_map_data$GEOID, "<br/>",
                "Diff Score: ",
                aim1_map_data %>% mutate(diff_score = c_A10_nyc - c_A10) %>% pull(diff_score)%>% round(2)
                
                ) %>%
            lapply(htmltools::HTML)

diff_pal <- colorNumeric(
  palette = colorRampPalette(c("blue", "white", "red"))(100),
  domain = c(-max(abs(aim1_map_data %>% mutate(diff_score = c_A10_nyc - c_A10) %>% pull(diff_score))), max(abs(aim1_map_data %>% mutate(diff_score = c_A10_nyc - c_A10) %>% pull(diff_score)))), # Symmetric range around 0
  na.color = "#808080"
)

aim1_map_data %>% 
  mutate_at(vars(w_A5:d_A1_nyc), ~replace_na(.,0)) %>% 
   mutate_at(vars(lwc_A2:lwc_A15_0.975), ~replace_na(.,0)) %>% 
  mutate(diff_score = c_A10_nyc - c_A10) %>% 
  sf::st_transform(crs = 4263) %>% 
leaflet() %>% 
  addProviderTiles(providers$CartoDB.Positron) %>% 
  addPolygons(
    fillColor = ~diff_pal(diff_score),
    fillOpacity = 0.7,
    color = "gray",  # Border color
    weight = 0.5,    # Border weight
    opacity = 1,
    highlightOptions = highlightOptions(
      weight = 2,
      color = "blue",
      fillOpacity = 0.9,
      bringToFront = TRUE
    ),
    label = diff_labels,
    labelOptions = labelOptions(
      style = list("font-weight" = "bold", "color" = "black"),
      textsize = "12px",
      direction = "auto"
    )
  ) %>% 
  addLegend(
    pal = diff_pal,
    values = ~diff_score %>% round(2),
    title = "Difference In Score",
    opacity = 1,
    position = "topright"
  )

 aim1_map_data %>% 
   mutate_at(vars(w_A5:d_A1_nyc), ~replace_na(.,0)) %>% 
   mutate_at(vars(lwc_A2:lwc_A15_0.975), ~replace_na(.,0)) %>% 
   mutate(diff_score = c_A10_nyc - c_A10) %>% 
   ggplot()+
   geom_histogram(aes(x = diff_score), fill = "blue", alpha = 0.3 )
```

```{r}
# scatter plots

aim1_map_data %>% 
  mutate(
         access_rank_w = case_when(
     w_A2 >= quantile(w_A2,0.75) ~ 1,
     w_A5 >= quantile(w_A5,0.75) ~ 2,
     w_A10 >= quantile(w_A10,0.75) ~ 3,
     w_A15 >= quantile(w_A15,0.75) ~ 4,
     w_A15 < quantile(w_A15,0.75) ~ 5
    
  ) %>% factor(),
  access_rank_c = case_when(
     c_A2 >= quantile(c_A2,0.75) ~ 1,
     c_A5 >= quantile(c_A5,0.75) ~ 2,
     c_A10 >= quantile(c_A10,0.75) ~ 3,
     c_A15 >= quantile(c_A15,0.75) ~ 4,
     c_A15 < quantile(c_A15,0.75) ~ 5
    
  ) %>% factor()
  ) %>% 
  ggplot()+
  geom_sf(aes(fill = access_rank_w))+
  scale_fill_viridis_d(direction = -1, option = "C")+
  aim1_map_data %>% 
  mutate(
         access_rank_w = case_when(
     w_A2 >= quantile(w_A2,0.75) ~ 1,
     w_A5 >= quantile(w_A5,0.75) ~ 2,
     w_A10 >= quantile(w_A10,0.75) ~ 3,
     w_A15 >= quantile(w_A15,0.75) ~ 4,
     w_A15 < quantile(w_A15,0.75) ~ 5
    
  ) %>% factor(),
  access_rank_c = case_when(
     c_A2 >= quantile(c_A2,0.75) ~ 1,
     c_A5 >= quantile(c_A5,0.75) ~ 2,
     c_A10 >= quantile(c_A10,0.75) ~ 3,
     c_A15 >= quantile(c_A15,0.75) ~ 4,
     c_A15 < quantile(c_A15,0.75) ~ 5
    
  ) %>% factor()
  ) %>% 
  ggplot()+
  geom_sf(aes(fill = access_rank_c))+
  scale_fill_viridis_d(direction = -1, option = "C")
```

```{r}
aim1_map_data %>% 
  mutate_at(vars(w_A5:d_A1_nyc), ~replace_na(.,0)) %>% 
   mutate_at(vars(lwc_A2:lwc_A15_0.975), ~replace_na(.,0)) %>% 
  mutate(car_prop = estimate_car_truck_van/estimate_total_trans ,
         non_car_prop = 1-car_prop,
         access_rank_w = case_when(
     w_A2*non_car_prop >= quantile(w_A2*non_car_prop,0.75, na.rm = T) ~ 1,
     w_A5*non_car_prop >= quantile(w_A5*non_car_prop,0.75, na.rm = T) ~ 2,
     w_A10*non_car_prop >= quantile(w_A10*non_car_prop,0.75, na.rm = T) ~ 3,
     w_A15*non_car_prop >= quantile(w_A15*non_car_prop,0.75, na.rm = T) ~ 4,
     w_A15*non_car_prop < quantile(w_A15*non_car_prop,0.75, na.rm = T) ~ 5
    
  ) %>% factor()
  ) %>% 
  ggplot()+
  geom_sf(aes(fill = access_rank_w))+
  scale_fill_viridis_d(direction = -1, option = "C")+
  aim1_map_data %>% 
  mutate(car_prop = estimate_car_truck_van/estimate_total_trans ,
         non_car_prop = 1-car_prop,
         
  access_rank_c = case_when(
     c_A2*car_prop >= quantile(c_A2*car_prop,0.75, na.rm = T) ~ 1,
     c_A5*car_prop >= quantile(c_A5*car_prop,0.75, na.rm = T) ~ 2,
     c_A10*car_prop >= quantile(c_A10*car_prop,0.75, na.rm = T) ~ 3,
     c_A15*car_prop >= quantile(c_A15*car_prop,0.75, na.rm = T) ~ 4,
     c_A15*car_prop < quantile(c_A15*car_prop,0.75, na.rm = T) ~ 5
    
  ) %>% factor()
  ) %>% 
  ggplot()+
  geom_sf(aes(fill = access_rank_c))+
  scale_fill_viridis_d(direction = -1, option = "C")
  aim1_map_data %>% 
   mutate(car_prop = estimate_car_truck_van/estimate_total_trans ,
         non_car_prop = 1-car_prop,
         a_rank_w = case_when(
     w_A2*non_car_prop >= quantile(w_A2*non_car_prop,0.75, na.rm = T) ~ 1,
     w_A5*non_car_prop >= quantile(w_A5*non_car_prop,0.75, na.rm = T) ~ 2,
     w_A10*non_car_prop >= quantile(w_A10*non_car_prop,0.75, na.rm = T) ~ 3,
     w_A15*non_car_prop >= quantile(w_A15*non_car_prop,0.75, na.rm = T) ~ 4,
     w_A15*non_car_prop < quantile(w_A15*non_car_prop,0.75, na.rm = T) ~ 5
    
  )# %>% factor()
  ,
   a_rank_c = case_when(
     c_A2*car_prop >= quantile(c_A2*car_prop,0.75, na.rm = T) ~ 1,
     c_A5*car_prop >= quantile(c_A5*car_prop,0.75, na.rm = T) ~ 2,
     c_A10*car_prop >= quantile(c_A10*car_prop,0.75, na.rm = T) ~ 3,
     c_A15*car_prop >= quantile(c_A15*car_prop,0.75, na.rm = T) ~ 4,
     c_A15*car_prop < quantile(c_A15*car_prop,0.75, na.rm = T) ~ 5
    
  )# %>% factor()
  ,
  a_rank_lwc = (a_rank_w + a_rank_c) %>%  factor()
      ) %>% 
  ggplot()+
  geom_sf(aes(fill = a_rank_lwc))+
  scale_fill_viridis_d(direction = -1, option = "C")
```

```{r}
aim1_map_data %>% 
  mutate_at(vars(w_A5:d_A1_nyc), ~replace_na(.,0)) %>% 
   mutate_at(vars(lwc_A2:lwc_A15_0.975), ~replace_na(.,0)) %>% 
  mutate(car_prop = estimate_car_truck_van/estimate_total_trans ,
         non_car_prop = 1-car_prop,
         access_rank_w = case_when(
     w_A2 >= quantile(w_A2,0.75, na.rm = T) ~ 1,
     w_A5 >= quantile(w_A5,0.75, na.rm = T) ~ 2,
     w_A10 >= quantile(w_A10,0.75, na.rm = T) ~ 3,
     w_A15 >= quantile(w_A15,0.75, na.rm = T) ~ 4,
     w_A15 < quantile(w_A15,0.75, na.rm = T) ~ 5
    
  ) %>% factor()
  ) %>% 
  ggplot()+
  geom_sf(aes(fill = access_rank_w))+
  scale_fill_viridis_d(direction = -1, option = "C")+
  aim1_map_data %>% 
  mutate(car_prop = estimate_car_truck_van/estimate_total_trans ,
         non_car_prop = 1-car_prop,
         
  access_rank_c = case_when(
     c_A2 >= quantile(c_A2,0.75, na.rm = T) ~ 1,
     c_A5 >= quantile(c_A5,0.75, na.rm = T) ~ 2,
     c_A10 >= quantile(c_A10,0.75, na.rm = T) ~ 3,
     c_A15 >= quantile(c_A15,0.75, na.rm = T) ~ 4,
     c_A15 < quantile(c_A15,0.75, na.rm = T) ~ 5
    
  ) %>% factor()
  ) %>% 
  ggplot()+
  geom_sf(aes(fill = access_rank_c))+
  scale_fill_viridis_d(direction = -1, option = "C")+


aim1_map_data %>% 
  mutate_at(vars(w_A5:d_A1_nyc), ~replace_na(.,0)) %>% 
   mutate_at(vars(lwc_A2:lwc_A15_0.975), ~replace_na(.,0)) %>% 
   mutate(car_prop = estimate_car_truck_van/estimate_total_trans ,
         non_car_prop = 1-car_prop,
          a_rank_w = case_when(
     w_A2 >= quantile(w_A2,0.75, na.rm = T) ~ 1,
     w_A5 >= quantile(w_A5,0.75, na.rm = T) ~ 2,
     w_A10 >= quantile(w_A10,0.75, na.rm = T) ~ 3,
     w_A15 >= quantile(w_A15,0.75, na.rm = T) ~ 4,
     w_A15 < quantile(w_A15,0.75, na.rm = T) ~ 5
    
  )# %>% factor()
  ,
  a_rank_c = case_when(
     c_A2 >= quantile(c_A2,0.75, na.rm = T) ~ 1,
     c_A5 >= quantile(c_A5,0.75, na.rm = T) ~ 2,
     c_A10 >= quantile(c_A10,0.75, na.rm = T) ~ 3,
     c_A15 >= quantile(c_A15,0.75, na.rm = T) ~ 4,
     c_A15 < quantile(c_A15,0.75, na.rm = T) ~ 5
    
  )# %>% factor()
  ,
  a_rank_lwc = (a_rank_w + a_rank_c) %>%  factor()
      ) %>% 
  ggplot()+
  geom_sf(aes(fill = a_rank_lwc))+
  scale_fill_viridis_d(direction = -1, option = "C")
```

```{r}

aim1_map_data %>% 
  mutate_at(vars(w_A5:d_A1_nyc), ~replace_na(.,0)) %>% 
   mutate_at(vars(lwc_A2:lwc_A15_0.975), ~replace_na(.,0)) %>% 
   mutate(car_prop = estimate_car_truck_van/estimate_total_trans ,
         non_car_prop = 1-car_prop,
          a_rank_w = case_when(
     w_A2 >= quantile(w_A2,0.75, na.rm = T) ~ 1,
     w_A5 >= quantile(w_A5,0.75, na.rm = T) ~ 2,
     w_A10 >= quantile(w_A10,0.75, na.rm = T) ~ 3,
     w_A15 >= quantile(w_A15,0.75, na.rm = T) ~ 4,
     w_A15 < quantile(w_A15,0.75, na.rm = T) ~ 5
    
  )# %>% factor()
  ,
  a_rank_c = case_when(
     c_A2 >= quantile(c_A2,0.75, na.rm = T) ~ 1,
     c_A5 >= quantile(c_A5,0.75, na.rm = T) ~ 2,
     c_A10 >= quantile(c_A10,0.75, na.rm = T) ~ 3,
     c_A15 >= quantile(c_A15,0.75, na.rm = T) ~ 4,
     c_A15 < quantile(c_A15,0.75, na.rm = T) ~ 5
    
  )# %>% factor()
  ,
  a_rank_lwc = (a_rank_w + a_rank_c) %>%  factor()
      ) %>% 
  ggplot()+
  geom_sf(aes(fill = a_rank_lwc))+
  scale_fill_viridis_d(direction = -1, option = "C")+
  aim1_map_data %>% 
   mutate(car_prop = estimate_car_truck_van/estimate_total_trans ,
         non_car_prop = 1-car_prop,
         a_rank_w = case_when(
     w_A2*non_car_prop >= quantile(w_A2*non_car_prop,0.75, na.rm = T) ~ 1,
     w_A5*non_car_prop >= quantile(w_A5*non_car_prop,0.75, na.rm = T) ~ 2,
     w_A10*non_car_prop >= quantile(w_A10*non_car_prop,0.75, na.rm = T) ~ 3,
     w_A15*non_car_prop >= quantile(w_A15*non_car_prop,0.75, na.rm = T) ~ 4,
     w_A15*non_car_prop < quantile(w_A15*non_car_prop,0.75, na.rm = T) ~ 5
    
  )# %>% factor()
  ,
   a_rank_c = case_when(
     c_A2*car_prop >= quantile(c_A2*car_prop,0.75, na.rm = T) ~ 1,
     c_A5*car_prop >= quantile(c_A5*car_prop,0.75, na.rm = T) ~ 2,
     c_A10*car_prop >= quantile(c_A10*car_prop,0.75, na.rm = T) ~ 3,
     c_A15*car_prop >= quantile(c_A15*car_prop,0.75, na.rm = T) ~ 4,
     c_A15*car_prop < quantile(c_A15*car_prop,0.75, na.rm = T) ~ 5
    
  )# %>% factor()
  ,
  a_rank_lwc = (a_rank_w + a_rank_c) %>%  factor()
      ) %>% 
  ggplot()+
  geom_sf(aes(fill = a_rank_lwc))+
  scale_fill_viridis_d(direction = -1, option = "C")

```

```{r}

aim1_map_data %>% 
  mutate_at(vars(w_A5:d_A1_nyc), ~replace_na(.,0)) %>% 
   mutate_at(vars(lwc_A2:lwc_A15_0.975), ~replace_na(.,0)) %>% 
  mutate(car_prop = estimate_car_truck_van/estimate_total_trans ,
         non_car_prop = 1-car_prop,
         access_rank_w = case_when(
     w_A2 >= quantile(w_A2,0.75) ~ 1,
     w_A5 >= quantile(w_A5,0.75) ~ 2,
     w_A10 >= quantile(w_A10,0.75) ~ 3,
     w_A15 >= quantile(w_A15,0.75) ~ 4,
     w_A15 < quantile(w_A15,0.75) ~ 5
    
  ) %>% scale(center = F),
  access_rank_c = case_when(
     c_A2 >= quantile(c_A2,0.75) ~ 1,
     c_A5 >= quantile(c_A5,0.75) ~ 2,
     c_A10 >= quantile(c_A10,0.75) ~ 3,
     c_A15 >= quantile(c_A15,0.75) ~ 4,
     c_A15 < quantile(c_A15,0.75) ~ 5
    
  ) %>% scale(center = F)
  ) %>% 
  ggplot()+
  geom_sf(aes(fill = access_rank_c))+
  scale_fill_viridis_c(direction = 1)


aim1_map_data %>% 
  mutate_at(vars(w_A5:d_A1_nyc), ~replace_na(.,0)) %>% 
   mutate_at(vars(lwc_A2:lwc_A15_0.975), ~replace_na(.,0)) %>% 
  mutate(car_prop = estimate_car_truck_van/estimate_total_trans,
         non_car_prop = 1-car_prop,
         median_access_walk = median(w_A5,na.rm = T),
         median_access_car = median(c_A5,na.rm = T),
         weighted_w_A = case_when(
           w_A5/median_access_walk == 1 ~ 0,
           w_A5/median_access_walk < 1 ~ 1,
           w_A5/median_access_walk > 1 ~ 2
           ) %>% factor()
         
  ) %>% 
  ggplot()+
  geom_sf(aes(fill = weighted_w_A))+
  scale_fill_viridis_d(direction = 1)
  
```

```{r}
aim1_map_data %>% plot_access_map("w_A2")

aim1_map_data %>% plot_access_map("lwc_10")

aim1_map_data %>% plot_access_map("c_A15")

aim1_map_data %>% plot_access_map("lwc_A5", color_option = "E")+
aim1_map_data %>% plot_access_map("lwc_A10", color_option = "E")

aim1_map_data %>% 
  ggplot()+
  geom_sf(aes(fill = lwc_A5 %>% log()))+
  scale_fill_viridis_c()+
aim1_map_data %>%
  ggplot()+
  geom_sf(aes(fill = lwc_A5_0.025 %>% log()))+
  scale_fill_viridis_c()+
aim1_map_data %>% 
  ggplot()+
  geom_sf(aes(fill = lwc_A5_0.975%>% log()))+
  scale_fill_viridis_c()

aim1_map_data %>% 
  plot_access_map("d_A0.5",color_option = "E")+
aim1_map_data %>% 
  mutate(diff1 = d_A0.61- d_A0.5,
         diff1_c = case_when(diff1 <= -1 ~ "Dec 1",
                             diff1 > -1 & diff1 < 1 ~ "No Diff",
                             diff1 >= 1 ~ "Inc 1")) %>% 
  ggplot()+
  geom_sf(aes(fill = diff1_c))+
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2")+
  theme(legend.position = "none")+
 aim1_map_data %>% 
  mutate(diff2 = d_A1- d_A0.61,
         diff2_c = case_when(diff2 <= -1 ~ "Dec 1",
                             diff2 > -1 & diff2 < 1 ~ "No Diff",
                             diff2 >= 1 ~ "Inc 1")) %>% 
  ggplot()+
  geom_sf(aes(fill = diff2_c))+
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2", name = "Change")+
  theme(legend.position = "right",
        axis.line.x = element_blank())

aim1_data %>% 
  select(
    geoid,
    w_A2,
         w_A5,
         w_A10,
         w_A15,
         c_A2,
         c_A5,
         c_A10,
         c_A15,
         lwc_A2,
         lwc_A5,
         lwc_A10,
         lwc_A15,
         d_A0.5,
         d_A0.61,
         d_A1) %>% 

  pivot_longer(
    w_A2:d_A1,
    values_to = "score",
    names_to = "name"
  ) %>% 
  ggplot()+
  geom_boxplot(aes(x = name, y = score, fill = name),outliers = F)

aim1_sf <- st_as_sf(aim1_map_data)  # Ensure it's an sf object

# Compute Queen's Contiguity neighbors
neighbors <- poly2nb(aim1_sf, queen = TRUE)

aim1_map_data %>% 
  mutate(neighbor_count = sapply(neighbors, length)) %>% 
  sf::st_drop_geometry() %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(GEOID,neighbor_count)))+
  geom_line(aes(y = lwc_A5))+
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )
```

### Interactive - LWC plots

```{r}

# Create custom HTML labels using a classification (Under, Fit, Over) based on lwc_A5 thresholds.
diff_labels_lwc5_u <- paste0(
  "Tract: ", aim1_map_data$GEOID, "<br/>",
  "LWC 5 min: ",
  aim1_map_data %>% 
    mutate(u = factor(case_when(
      lwc_A5_0.025 <= lwc_A5 - 1 ~ "Under",
      lwc_A5_0.975 >= lwc_A5 + 1 ~ "Over",
      lwc_A5_0.025 > lwc_A5 - 1 & lwc_A5_0.975 < lwc_A5 + 1 ~ "Fit"
    ), levels = c("Under", "Fit", "Over"))) %>% 
    pull(u)
) %>% lapply(htmltools::HTML)

# Define a factor color palette based on the three levels.
diff_pal_lw5_u <- colorFactor(
  palette = colorRampPalette(c("blue", "gray85", "red"))(3),
  domain = aim1_map_data %>% 
    mutate(u = factor(case_when(
      lwc_A5_0.025 <= lwc_A5 - 1 ~ "Under",
      lwc_A5_0.975 >= lwc_A5 + 1 ~ "Over",
      lwc_A5_0.025 > lwc_A5 - 1 & lwc_A5_0.975 < lwc_A5 + 1 ~ "Fit"
    ), levels = c("Under", "Fit", "Over"))) %>% 
    pull(u),
  na.color = "#808080"
)

# Build the leaflet map using the factor variable for fill.
aim1_map_data %>% 
  mutate(u = case_when(
    lwc_A5_0.025 <= lwc_A5 - 1 ~ "Under",
    lwc_A5_0.975 >= lwc_A5 + 1 ~ "Over",
    lwc_A5_0.025 > lwc_A5 - 1 & lwc_A5_0.975 < lwc_A5 + 1 ~ "Fit"
  ) %>% forcats::fct_relevel(c("Under", "Fit", "Over"))) %>% 
  sf::st_transform(crs = 4263) %>% 
  leaflet() %>% 
  addProviderTiles(providers$CartoDB.Positron) %>% 
  addPolygons(
    fillColor = ~diff_pal_lw5_u(u),
    fillOpacity = 0.7,
    color = "gray",    # Border color
    weight = 0.5,      # Border weight
    opacity = 1,
    highlightOptions = highlightOptions(
      weight = 2,
      color = "blue",
      fillOpacity = 0.9,
      bringToFront = TRUE
    ),
    label = diff_labels_lwc5_u,
    labelOptions = labelOptions(
      style = list("font-weight" = "bold", "color" = "black"),
      textsize = "12px",
      direction = "auto"
    )
  ) %>% 
  addLegend(
    pal = diff_pal_lw5_u,
    values = ~u,
    title = "LWC 5 min",
    opacity = 1,
    position = "topright"
  )



```

```{r}


# Create custom HTML labels using a classification of true zero (A_0.025 < 1) based on lwc_A5 thresholds.
diff_labels_lwc5_u <- paste0(
  "Tract: ", aim1_map_data$GEOID, "<br/>",
  "Pop: ", aim1_map_data$population, "<br/>",
  "LWC 5 min: ",
  aim1_map_data %>% 
    mutate(u = factor(case_when(
      lwc_A5_0.025 < 1 ~ "zero",
      lwc_A5_0.025 <= lwc_A5 - 1 ~ "Under",
      lwc_A5_0.975 >= lwc_A5 + 1 ~ "Over",
      lwc_A5_0.025 > lwc_A5 - 1 & lwc_A5_0.975 < lwc_A5 + 1 ~ "Fit"
    ), levels = c("zero", "Under", "Fit", "Over"))) %>% 
    pull(u)
) %>% lapply(htmltools::HTML)

# Define a factor color palette based on the three levels.
diff_pal_lw5_u <- colorFactor(
  palette = colorRampPalette(c("black", "blue", "gray85", "red"))(4),
  domain = aim1_map_data %>% 
    mutate(u = factor(case_when(
      lwc_A5_0.025 < 1 ~ "zero",
      lwc_A5_0.025 <= lwc_A5 - 1 ~ "Under",
      lwc_A5_0.975 >= lwc_A5 + 1 ~ "Over",
      lwc_A5_0.025 > lwc_A5 - 1 & lwc_A5_0.975 < lwc_A5 + 1 ~ "Fit"
    ), levels = c("zero", "Under", "Fit", "Over"))) %>% 
    pull(u),
  na.color = "#808080"
)

# Build the leaflet map using the factor variable for fill.
aim1_map_data %>% 
  mutate(u = case_when(
     lwc_A5_0.025 < 1 ~ "zero",
    lwc_A5_0.025 <= lwc_A5 - 1 ~ "Under",
    lwc_A5_0.975 >= lwc_A5 + 1 ~ "Over",
    lwc_A5_0.025 > lwc_A5 - 1 & lwc_A5_0.975 < lwc_A5 + 1 ~ "Fit"
  ) %>% forcats::fct_relevel(c("zero", "Under", "Fit", "Over"))) %>% 
  sf::st_transform(crs = 4263) %>% 
  leaflet() %>% 
  addProviderTiles(providers$CartoDB.Positron) %>% 
  addPolygons(
    fillColor = ~diff_pal_lw5_u(u),
    fillOpacity = 0.7,
    color = "gray",    # Border color
    weight = 0.5,      # Border weight
    opacity = 1,
    highlightOptions = highlightOptions(
      weight = 2,
      color = "blue",
      fillOpacity = 0.9,
      bringToFront = TRUE
    ),
    label = diff_labels_lwc5_u,
    labelOptions = labelOptions(
      style = list("font-weight" = "bold", "color" = "black"),
      textsize = "12px",
      direction = "auto"
    )
  ) %>% 
  addLegend(
    pal = diff_pal_lw5_u,
    values = ~u,
    title = "LWC 5 min",
    opacity = 1,
    position = "topright"
  )

# For lwc_A2
aim1_map_data %>% 
  mutate(u = case_when(
    lwc_A2_0.025 <= lwc_A2 - 1 ~ "Under",
    lwc_A2_0.975 >= lwc_A2 + 1 ~ "Over",
    lwc_A2_0.025 >  lwc_A2 - 1 & lwc_A2_0.975 <  lwc_A2 + 1 ~ "Fit"
```
